_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to identify and describe how texts are structured differently depending on their purpose, and how language features vary across different types of texts.

_source.w: We are learning to identify and describe how texts are structured differently depending on their purpose, and how language features vary across different types of texts.
y: Deep understanding of text structures helps us comprehend complex texts and create our own effectively.
Recognising how language features serve purpose builds our analytical and writing skills.
This knowledge transfers to every subject where we encounter or create different text types.
eg: We analyse the structure of an analytical essay and compare it with a narrative structure.
We describe how language features in a scientific report differ from those in a persuasive speech.
We identify the stages of a discussion text and explain how each stage serves the text's purpose.
We examine how texts that combine purposes — like an informative text with a persuasive angle — are structured.
We compare the structures of texts from different media — print, digital, broadcast.
We use our understanding of text structures to plan and produce more effective texts.
